LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT CONTINUES TO FIND THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT STATE A CLAIM FOR NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. THING V. LA CHUSA (1989) 48 CAL. 3D 644; BIRD V. SAENZ (2002) 28 CAL 4TH 910 AND GOLDSTEIN V. SUPERIOR COURT (1990) 223 CAL. APP. 3D 1415. THE COMPLAINT FAILS TO ALLEGE FACTS THAT PROVIDE A BASIS FOR THE CONCLUSION THAT A LAY PERSON COULD PERCIEVE A MISDIAGNOSIS OR FAILURE TO DIAGNOSE THE PRESENCE OF A SPONGE IN THE CHILD'S ABDOMEN. SEE MEIGHAN V. SHORE (1995) 34 CAL. APP. 4TH 1025. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Off Calendar Mar-02-2010 Off Calendar

Hearing On The Petition To Approve The Compromise Of A Disputed Claim Of A Minor.
Off Calendar Jan-14-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of a Minor.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Troy A. Brines, Esq. appeared for petitioner Amy Sexton, the Guardian Ad Litem for the minor Jackson Roybal.
Order waiving the appearance of both the minor and guardian was filed on January 13, 2010.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved.

Settlement Amount - $25,000.00.
Attorney's Fees - $6,250.00.
Costs - $8,750.00.

The net balance of $10,000.00 shall be used to purchase an annuity as described in Attachemt 7b(2)(b) of the Order Approving the Compromise.

Order Approving the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of a Minor was submitted to and signed by the Court.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-05-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances:
Stanley Wessels of Davidovitz & Bennett (956.4800) for defendant and moving party
Troy Brenes of Lopez & McHugh (949.737.1501) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: the demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The Court continues to find that plaintiff cannot state a claim for negligent infliction of emotional distress. Thing v. La Chusa (1989) 48 Cal. 3d 644; Bird v. Saenz (2002) 28 Cal 4th 910 and Goldstein v. Superior Court (1990) 223 Cal. App. 3d 1415. The complaint fails to allege facts that provide a basis for the conclusion that a lay person could percieve a misdiagnosis or failure to diagnose the presence of a sponge in the child's abdomen. See Meighan v. Shore (1995) 34 Cal. App. 4th 1025. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
